claim: Marimo notebooks have reactive execution: when you interact with UI elements or modify code, Marimo automatically determines which downstream cells need to re-run
actionable: Use Marimo's reactive execution model for data workflows: build dependent cells that automatically update when upstream data changes, avoiding manual re-execution
source_title: The Future of Notebooks - with Akshay Agrawal of Marimo

claim: Marimo notebooks enforce functional style by preventing variable redefinition across cells, reducing hidden state and reproducibility issues endemic to Jupyter
actionable: When migrating from Jupyter to Marimo, adopt functional programming patterns: define variables once per cell and use immutable transformations, which Marimo enforces automatically
source_title: The Future of Notebooks - with Akshay Agrawal of Marimo

claim: Marimo notebooks can be executed as scripts (marimo run), deployed as read-only web apps (marimo run), or versioned with git because they are stored as pure Python
actionable: Choose Marimo over Jupyter if you need to both prototype in a notebook and ship as a production data app or script without rewriting
source_title: The Future of Notebooks - with Akshay Agrawal of Marimo

claim: Marimo has tight integration with UV package manager, allowing dependencies to be stored inline in notebook headers, making notebooks reproducible and shareable
actionable: Use Marimo's UV integration to embed dependencies directly in notebook files so users can pip install a single notebook file with all transitive dependencies resolved
source_title: The Future of Notebooks - with Akshay Agrawal of Marimo

claim: Marimo has native AI code generation with context awareness: when you tag variables with @df, it passes column names and data types to the LLM for richer completions
actionable: When using AI to generate code in Marimo, tag relevant dataframes and objects with @ to pass rich context including column names and types for better LLM outputs
source_title: The Future of Notebooks - with Akshay Agrawal of Marimo

claim: Marimo supports multi-column layouts and cell execution order based on variable dependencies (like Excel), enabling non-linear notebook designs
actionable: Use Marimo's column layout feature and dependency-driven execution to create side-by-side comparisons or dashboards without managing cell execution order manually
source_title: The Future of Notebooks - with Akshay Agrawal of Marimo

claim: Marimo supports Web Assembly execution and a cloud-hosted notebook service (MoLab) for seamless sharing without requiring local Python environments
actionable: Use MoLab (marimo's cloud-hosted notebooks) for sharing analyses with non-technical stakeholders or for notebooks requiring heavy package dependencies
source_title: The Future of Notebooks - with Akshay Agrawal of Marimo

claim: Marimo can be converted from Jupyter notebooks using the command `marimo convert notebook.ipynb notebook.py` with automated variable redefinition fixes
actionable: Migrate Jupyter projects to Marimo by running the conversion command; review the output for any local variable patterns that need adjustment
source_title: The Future of Notebooks - with Akshay Agrawal of Marimo
